title: Pentacalia carchiensis
intro: Pentacalia carchiensis, also known as Carchi's pentacalia, is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia carchiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the preparation of tinctures and ointments for topical application.
taxonomy: Pentacalia carchiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentacalia and is a member of the tribe Senecioneae. The species is native to Ecuador and is part of the plant family that also includes dandelions and sunflowers. The classification of Pentacalia carchiensis is a result of ongoing research and the continuous reorganization of plant taxonomy. This species is considered to be an important part of the Asteraceae family, which comprises over 23,000 species of plants.
